response:
SECTION 1  
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"  

SECTION 2  
The person might express shock and disbelief, perhaps saying, "This cannot be true, sir—I am innocent." They might plead for a re-examination of the evidence, hoping it would lead to a fair resolution. A stranger entering the room might offer a silver lining, suggesting there was still time to wrap up the case properly. The innocent individual would likely seek clarity, support, and a chance to clear their name.
